source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Brokerage Cost Management: Delta Exchange charges high fees. Rule: Do scalping within 15 minutes (zero brokerage fee). Intraday trades over 15 minutes incur heavy fees. Example: ₹1.5 lakh trade → ~₹25,000 in fees lost. Strategy: Instead of 3-4 intraday trades risking 1% each (3% total + high fees), take 1-2 higher timeframe trades (4H, 1H, Daily) with 2-3% risk and let them run for 1-3 days. This reduces brokerage costs dramatically while maintaining similar risk exposure. Swing trading direction is preferred over scalping on Delta Exchange.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Optimal Trading Timing: Best times to trade crypto: 1) 5:30 PM to 10:00 PM (US session) - high volatility and volume. 2) 9:00 PM to 11:00 PM (overlap of US evening + Tokyo morning opening). 3) Morning 5:30 AM to 7:00 AM (Tokyo session opening, daily candle changes at 5:30 AM IST). Price nature changes throughout the day - these 3 windows have the clearest moves. Avoid trading during London-Tokyo overlap (8:30 AM - 12:30 PM IST) as price often consolidates. Saturday-Sunday: reduced volatility, price tends to retrace.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Session-Based Trading Strategy: 4 global sessions: Tokyo (5:30 AM IST), London (12:30 PM IST), New York (7:00 PM IST), Sydney (2:30 AM IST). For crypto: Focus on 2 key sessions - Tokyo (5:30 AM) and New York (7:00 PM). Why? 1) US market drives crypto sentiment. When US traders buy stocks/bonds, they also buy crypto. 2) After US market closes, Sydney opens (low volume) then Tokyo opens (high volume). Strategy: Take trades either during Tokyo closing (1:30 PM IST) or US opening (7:00 PM IST). Both windows give 1 hour of high-probability setups.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Multi-Timeframe Analysis (Top-Down): Start with Weekly → Daily → 4H → 1H → enter on 5-min or 15-min. Weekly/Daily: Determine overall trend direction. 4H: Identify key support/resistance levels. 1H: Find trend change signals using Double AI Super Trend indicator. 5-min: Entry and stop loss placement. Never go below 5-minute timeframe - too much noise. The 1-hour timeframe is where trend changes get confirmed. Below 1H = decision-making points only, not trend determination.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Indicator Setup: Double AI Super Trend - AI-based, self-adjusting indicator. Uses EMA (Exponential Moving Average) as base. Cumulative algorithm that improves with each signal generation. Green = bullish, Red = bearish. Plot on 4H and 1H for trend identification. When 4H turns from green to red → look for short entries on 1H. When both 4H and 1H are red → strong bearish confirmation. Warning: This is NOT a traditional indicator - it's AI-based and self-correcting with each generation.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Position Sizing & Leverage: Constant rule: Fix your risk in currency terms first, THEN calculate position size. Example: ₹2 lakh capital, 1% risk = ₹2,000. Stop loss = $50 → Position size = ₹2,000 / ₹4,250 (50 × 85) = ~0.47 lots. Leverage doesn't change your fixed loss - only changes margin used. 100x = ₹800 margin. 200x = ₹400 margin. Your LOSS remains ₹2,000 regardless. For beginners: Use 25x leverage for safety. Switch to isolated margin mode. Never use cross margin for new trades - it risks your entire account.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Pyramiding (Scaling In): Instead of averaging DOWN (buying more as price falls), use pyramiding UP. Example: Short at 114,370 → when price drops to 112,000 (in your favor), add more short positions. Each new position = smaller size. This increases position size in profitable trades, not losing ones. Most beginners do OPPOSITE - they keep buying in hoping price reverses = dead weight trades. Golden rule: When trade is working, scale in. When trade is against you, exit - don't add.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Stop Loss & TP/SL Orders: ALWAYS use Take Profit (TP) and Stop Loss (SL) orders. There are two types: 1) Cross Margin: If SL fails, your ENTIRE account balance at risk. Only use if you understand the risk. 2) Isolated Margin: Only the trade's margin at risk at most. For 20x leverage with ₹800 margin = max loss ₹800. Switch from Cross to Isolated in margin settings. Beginners MUST use isolated mode. One mistake with cross margin = account gone. When trade goes in your favor, trail the stop loss upward. Move SL to break-even once price moves 1:1 in your favor.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Liquidation Risk Education: Bitcoin CAN crash from $10,000 to $3,000 in ONE candle (70% drop in hours). This happened in April 2020. No circuit breaker exists for crypto. Your trade can go 50% against you in one move. With 100x leverage on ₹2,000 with ₹100 margin: 1% price move = liquidation. Solution: Always use stop loss. Trade with isolated margin. Position size appropriately. Never bet more than you can lose. The market doesn't care about your analysis - always protect capital first.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Decision-Making in Trading: 1) Don't wait for 'perfect' entry - wait for 'good enough' setup with plan. 2) Pre-plan every trade: entry, stop loss, target - all before clicking buy/sell. 3) 'No regrets' approach: If you followed your plan, no regret even if trade loses. 4) Don't think about liquidation price - think about your stop loss. If SL is set properly, liquidation is irrelevant. 5) Trading is about consistency, not home runs. Small profits + small losses with positive expectancy wins long-term. Most beginners fail because they skip steps: no plan, no SL, no position sizing.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Binance vs Delta Exchange: Binance: Better for primary trading. Bank deposits/withdrawals supported. More liquid (BTC spot). Lower slippage. Link bank account directly - no P2P hassle needed. Delta Exchange: Higher fees for intraday. Better for specific futures instruments. NOT recommended for beginners due to high brokerage costs. Recommendation: Use Binance for actual trading. Use TradingView for chart analysis (free version allows 2 indicators simultaneously by using 2 separate chart tabs).

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Candlestick Analysis forEntry Confidence: IntoRejection candle example: When price makes a new high (higher high) but closes lower than the open = sellers dominated. Bigger rejection = stronger signal. Compare rejection candles between timeframes: 4H rejection shows stronger conviction than Daily timeframe rejection. If 4H shows large bearish rejection but Daily shows small/weak rejection = sellers are weaker on higher timeframe = less confidence in short. Always confirm with higher timeframe structure before entering on lower timeframe.

source: youtube-interviews/Crypto Trading MASTERCLASS It could be strategy for 90 Accuracy.md
content: Real Performance Metrics (Last 30 days): 22 trades placed. 72% win rate. Average win: ₹11,000. Average loss: ₹1,600. Risk-reward average: 1:2.2. Many trades ran for 1-3 days (swing style). Goal with ₹2 lakh capital: Earn ₹1 lakh/month (50% monthly return). If achieved consistently = life-changing. Key insight: With discipline and proper process, even modest accounts can grow exponentially through compounding. Worst month still profitable. Drawdown managed through strict position sizing.
